reviews.review_msg: No Comments
She is good,
Liam was a good prof and his lectures were understandable, he was just kind of boring. Decide on what your final project will be early, it'll give you some breathing room in the future.

Liam was very approachable and kind, which made it easy to ask questions and seek help. The class, however, was broad and jumped around a lot. The assignments were self-driven, allowing students to explore topics they found interesting. Overall, Liam was positive and supportive, always complimenting ideas and encouraging students.

Liam was amazing prof! however, the assignments were not really based on anything relevant in the course. the assignments were all citation based and my TA was a harsh grader and would take marks off for something they never clarified. while he is great, there was no point to go to class since the homework was based on nothing we learnt. he is cool
Great guy, hes pretty funny but the class is a little bit boring. TAs are easy markers and dont expect much. Good class for gpa booster.

At least in my experience he was not racist. I'm not sure how he was racist to students since he was a pretty nice professor, but like any course you really need to read the material and put in your work. Do that and you should be fine. He was pretty chill in my experience.

Liam is very considerate with his feedback and makes his best effort for you to learn. As long as you read the readings before tutorial and have an opinion to be heard, you will be fine. He actually made learning about Canada more interesting than I expected. Hard to find nice profs like him, if you have the chance take his class !!

Admission tickets that you hand in before tutorials and if you forget to do it the 2nd time you won't get participation marks for that day (total worth: 20% of final grade). Lectures are extremely dry, quizzes are short but also stupid because she barely mentions quiz content in lecture. Grading criteria sucks. Not recommended as a GPA booster.

She's a passionate prof. However, she doesn't have any lecture slides for you because all of her "lecture slides" are just pictures. She never recognizes how fast she is talking yet she expects everyone to write down everything she says because what she says will be on the pop quiz. Textbook won't help you.

Lectures were extremely dry. Assignments had little to do with the lectures; they could be done without attending a single one. Marking criteria was vague and hard to follow. Readings were usually bland and unnecessarily long. Sweet and caring when help is needed, but not a great lecturer.

She is a lovely lady, but certainly not my favourite prof. Lectures were disorganized, (often) irrelevant, and difficult to follow. It also seemed like she tried to use up class time by having various historians come in and speak- only one of which I found relevant to the course material. Also irrelevant group work. Overall, very mediocre course
She walks around class all the times. she has a straight voice. this course is relatively easy but lots of reading.

Very passionate. Lectures weren't useful, as a lot of time was spent reading the text or conducting group discussions, which isn't going very productive with a class like this. Text book is necessary for tutorial participation and little else. Class doesn't really teach much, if you have basic writing skills you're likely guaranteed a B minimum.

It was really hard to follow her lectures and understand what was important. She wastes a lot of time in lectures by dividing the class to do group discussions. Some of her assignments were really pointless.
Good man!!

keeps the class humorous so it isn't too bad going to tutorials... really easy-going and really nice.. keeps the class lively and after you come out of class, you understand the article that was for homework.. even if you don't read it...   ..ever.
Easy going, hands out candy...just go to tutorials. Really helpful in office hours.

Course has a lot of information and a lot of work for a first year course. I took this as an elective to raise my gpa and sadly the only thing it did was drop it. TA was an incredibly picky and hard marker. Tried reaching out for some assistance and professor was rude and very blunt in telling me to help myself.

Wouldn't say HIST 101 is the greatest class to take with Prof Keough as her lectures are pre-recorded (Asynchronous) and can get quite dry/boring. The tutorial was super chill if you have a good TA. Lots of reading, but Ta's went over it during tutorials (Make sure you went to get answers) Otherwise, pretty easy, only 2 big papers and one final.

Prof. Keough is a caring and passionate prof, though her lectures are based mainly from the textbook (which I didn't use - lectures and articles are enough if you pay attention & take notes). The course is heavy and dry at times. Optional live discussions are more fun than the mandatory discussion board posts. If possible, in-person >>> online.

Dr. Keough was tough but fair. This course may be a bit heavy for first years, but overall it was not terribly difficult. Do NOT bother going to tutorials. They are not required and they are a waste of time, but I do recommend attending office hours. Overall a solid course.

Not really impressed with the amount of work that was expected for this class. It's a first-year class in a pandemic but it didn't feel like it. Not to mention due to our TA's error, our participation grades had to be curved down, awesome.

Dont take! Does not use appropriate language/context for Indigenous People and the max you will get on an assignment is 80-89%. Also, considering we are in a pandemic profs were expected to lighten the course load and she did NOT! She started a week early and expected students to write 3 essays as a first year final in a short time frame.
Fair grader and is very approachable.
Good TA - knows his stuff!

Eh its a TA for tutorial not professor. Tutorials usually are boring no matter the course, no ones fault. For this class it wasnt bad, good info and related to the course, helped make sense of things, well organized

Unusually hard grading for a first-year class. However, his biggest sin was his trying to overcompensate by "rewarding" students with higher participation marks only for them to be curved downwards after they were "too high." Not cool, especially during a pandemic in a FIRST-year class.
No Comments
